extra.reasoning: The property is monotonicity on the input `hemoptysis`: toggling it from false to true (all other inputs fixed) must never lower `acuity`. Scanning all 8 rules (lines 17-24), `hemoptysis` is declared as an input (line 11) but is not referenced in the condition of ANY rule. The rules only fire on fever, chest_pain, stiff_neck, palpitations, back_pain, abdominal_pain, dyspnea, and confusion. The single output `acuity` is determined by priority-based conflict resolution over those rules plus the default (routine). Since `hemoptysis` is a completely unused input, changing its value does not change which rules match, does not change priorities, and does not change the default — so the computed `acuity` is identical before and after toggling hemoptysis. Identical acuity satisfies "no lower than before" (equality meets the >= condition). Therefore the property holds vacuously across the entire input space; no input or execution can violate it.
